data.title: Axiata Group: Patrick Forth Is Senior Adviser at Boston Consulting Group
FNC INTERVIEW WITH PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

March 28, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL

FNC INTERVIEW WITH PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

FNC INTERVIEW WITH PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL

MARCH 28, 2025

SPEAKERS:

PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL

LAURA INGRAHAM, FNC HOST

LAURA INGRAHAM: But joining us now to discuss all of this for the first time on the Angle, the Attorney General Pam Bondi. General Bondi, it's so good to see you. How did you find this guy, and how long has he been in the United States?

PAM BONDI (R), ATTORNEY GENERAL: He has been in the United States for probably over a decade, but we believe he was recruited in middle school. In middle school, can you believe that? In middle school, but he's been living here illegally with friends and family, and the Biden administration did absolutely nothing to deport these people. You know, as the President says we didn't need, and we didn't need new laws. We needed a new president to fix this.

And today, with Governor Glenn Youngkin and a task force, we started it February 25th and in less than a month, they have captured so many of these MS-13 gang members. Laura, their techniques and what they were doing with state, local, federal partnerships hasn't been done.

INGRAHAM: People are watching this tonight, they're saying, how on earth could this have happened? Not only this, which was significant, obviously, 24 years of age, very young and very vicious. Then you have these people in Massachusetts that ICE has rounded up, these child predators and rapists who you know, excuse me, mayor of Boston and the governor of Massachusetts. Oh no, we're tough on criminals. These people are just walking about the streets free as a bird until you guys actually got them.

BONDI: They are.

INGRAHAM: It's insane.

BONDI: They are. So this task force is DEA, FBI, U.S. Marshals working with ICE, all working hand in hand sharing data, and that's how we were able to catch this guy. One of the agents said, if you want to rock MS-13 to its core, arrest him. That shows how bad he was. He was one of the top leaders, and he was living half an hour from where we're sitting —

INGRAHAM: Dale City.

BONDI: — right now. Living among us.

INGRAHAM: How did he get that high up in the organization, if he's only 24 years of age, you know.

BONDI: He's been in it for a decade, a decade this guy has been in MS-13 —

INGRAHAM: Do you think he's murdered anyone?

BONDI: I think he's probably been involved in murder —

INGRAHAM: Order murders.

BONDI: — more to come on that. They found letters. They found writings that show he was one of the top leaders for the whole country, showing MS-13 clicks, which mean gangs spread throughout this nation. Again, we took one of the worst of the worst off the streets today, Americans are safer because of Donald Trump.

INGRAHAM: When can we expect federal charges? And will you try to deport him to the Supermax prison back in his home country of El Salvador?

BONDI: Yes, we will try to deport him to El Salvador. Let's send him home. That's where he belongs. You know, he belongs back in that El Salvador prison, and that's where he should go. But we're going to — you're going to see a lot more charges on him. He had illegal firearms, of course, four to five guns. He was living in a middle —

INGRAHAM: Did he go to school?

BONDI: — class neighborhood.

INGRAHAM: Did he go to school in that area?

BONDI: He did when he was younger. He went to school there, which is where he was recruited. And you know what, these schools have been trying to protect kids from law enforcement —

INGRAHAM: Explain that and that's still happening in the United States. That log jam has to be broken because they're acting in some cases, not all, but in some cases, with the help of some religious leaders as sanctuaries from you and the FBI and the DHS.

BONDI: That's right, kids are being recruited in middle school. They're 13 years old, 14 years old, yet these schools will not let law enforcement officers in there to educate kids about this, how horrible gangs are. No longer will that happen. Governor Youngkin and I are going to work together on that, and we are going to get into these schools. No longer will these schools be keeping law enforcement from children trying to help them.

INGRAHAM: There was this other MS-13 character, Cesar Humberto Lopez-Larios, many names. He was arrested on terrorism charges last year, but then CNN is reporting that, I guess it was dropped days ago, that he was back to El Salvador, sent back to the Supermax prison and but is it a mistake to have sent him there? I think there are a couple instances where maybe they weren't supposed to be sent there?

BONDI: We have the people who have been deported, who are being housed in El Salvador should be there. 261 people total went on that plane. And that's 261 reasons why Americans are safer tonight.

INGRAHAM: What are you going to do about these nationwide injunctions? And what is your message tonight to Congress, specifically members of the U.S. Senate and House, but who — Republicans and Democrats, who like to leave town on Thursday night, tonight, and they leave and they're going to be gone for two weeks in April, when you still have a lot of key people, not in top positions. What is your message to them?

BONDI: We need these people, of course —

INGRAHAM: Should they be taking a recess for two weeks?

BONDI: Of course not. We — Laura, we need — we need to get all of — Donald Trump needs all of his nominees to be confirmed to be in office for public safety reasons, for all of the reasons we've been talking about. There are so many nominees that we have to get confirmed. We have a lot coming up, and we've got to get them in office to help keep America safe.

INGRAHAM: I think Thune is trying to do his best, but he has to have unanimous consent from the Democrats in order to fast track these and because Schumer is under attack from the Left, he's like, I'm not giving you a thumbs up on any of these people. So it's having to go through this laborious process. But if you keep people through the weekends, let's say you know this town, you keep people through a couple weekends. What does that do to them?

BONDI: They cave.

INGRAHAM: They break.

BONDI: The Democrats will break.

INGRAHAM: Yes.

BONDI: And Republicans are tougher. We know that. Senator Thune has been great. I mean, he's been fabulous, but we've got to keep people here. We've got to get people —

INGRAHAM: Stay.

BONDI: — office.

INGRAHAM: What's happening with this Las Vegas Tesla criminal who — he was arrested, and now federal charges have been filed against him. This was something that was needed, given everything we're seeing happening to those dealerships and those cars across the country.

BONDI: That was remarkable police work, working with the state police, our FBI, they have been going non-stop on this case. This guy is in custody. Laura, he is facing five year minimum mandatory to 20 years in prison. And this was great police work. This guy thought he got away with it. So these people better look out. They better cut it out, because we are coming after you, and at my direction, there will be no negotiating on these people —

INGRAHAM: No plea deals.

BONDI: — we are seeking 20 years in prison. They are setting off bombs, the huge fires. Look what you're seeing, huge, massive fires in residential neighborhoods —

INGRAHAM: Someone's going get killed.

BONDI: — charging stations. Someone is going to get killed, a citizen is going to get killed. And these people, we are not doing any plea negotiations on them. Well, we better cut it out.

INGRAHAM: We have some information about the groups that he belonged to —

BONDI: Yes.

INGRAHAM: — this individual. Let's watch.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

DORI KOREN, ASSISTANT SHERIFF, THE LAS VEGAS METROPOLITAN

POLICE DEPARTMENT: But a preliminary assessment of the suspect's social media activity indicates some very loose but self-proclaimed ties to the Communist Party USA social media group as well as social media groups called Revolutionary Communist International, Hidden Palestine, Palestine Action and a variety of other social media groups.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

INGRAHAM: I mean, this is like a cliche, a very Left-wing communist, socialist, Hamas, right?

BONDI: We are looking at, in all of these cases, every aspect, every — we are not going to leave a stone unturned —

INGRAHAM: So the messages are going out —

(CROSSTALK)

INGRAHAM: — Madam Attorney General, from these groups, get busy. Get busy meaning terrorize people — people are terrorized driving down the road in a car that they worked hard for. They feel terrorized.

BONDI: They do. And we're looking at how these people learned to make these bombs. We're looking at every aspect of these cases, and we are coming after you, and we will find you if you have done this to a Tesla owner, a Tesla dealership. Anything to do with Tesla, we're going to find you, and that today was proof of it.

INGRAHAM: Judge Beryl Howell is an Obama appointee district judge, I believe, in the district of D.C., District of Columbia, and is extremely upset at what he thinks is a series of attacks by the DOJ on the judiciary, saying, when the DOJ engages in this rhetorical strategy of ad hominem attack, the stakes become much larger than only the reputation of the targeted federal judge.

This strategy is designed to impugn the integrity of the federal judicial system. I guess the DOJ lawyers wanted to remove judge Howell from the case, citing other what they believed were biased comments, your reaction to Judge Howell tonight.

BONDI: We do. Many judges need to be removed. Judge Howell included, Judge Reyes, Judge Boasberg. These judges obviously cannot be impartial. They cannot be objective. They are district judges trying to control our entire country, our entire country and they are trying to obstruct Donald Trump's agenda.

INGRAHAM: And Judge Boasberg is a busy judge. He was assigned to the Signalgate, which is such a ridiculous name, but the Signal chat case, which is a lawsuit filed by parties which I'm not sure they have standing to file, but President Trump calls it disgraceful that this judge, again, who's ruled against you on the Tren de Aragua deportations —

BONDI: Yes.

INGRAHAM: — is now assigned to the Signal case. Your reaction tonight to that.

BONDI: Laura, he has multiple cases, I guess it's a wild coincidence against Donald Trump and our administration and now he has the Signal case. He shouldn't be on any of these cases. He cannot be objective. He's made that crystal clear. These judges are taking personal attacks to it at many of the cabinet secretaries and these judges across the country. And again, they think they have authority, but it's going to be short lived, because these cases are going to get to the Supreme Court very fast, doing everything we can.

INGRAHAM: CNN is — there Elie Honig, sometimes it's fair, but, you know, mostly bias, so you always have to open up a Signalgate investigation at DOJ. Watch this.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

ELIE HONIG, CNN SENIOR LEGAL ANALYST: So Pam Bondi has been MIA. There is no indication that she's going to open an investigation here. And by the way, when she was up for a confirmation as A.G. remember, one of the concerns is, would she be able to exercise independence? She told the Senate, absolutely, I will be completely independent. There's no reasonable prosecutor who could look at this and say it doesn't even need to be investigated.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

INGRAHAM: Your response to your pal there?

BONDI: Yes, there is a reason why their ratings are plummeting, because they're putting people like that on TV. Chatter, we could care less what they say. We're going to continue forward on all of these cases.

INGRAHAM: So the — this is not a concern of yours, inspector general, you know idea of inspector general investigation or congressional investigation down the road, you are completely confident that this was — this was a mistake, and nothing more than that.

BONDI: We are. And all the — all of our intelligence officials who were on it are confident of that as well.

INGRAHAM: I mean do you not know like how many Democrats that I know over the years have been on Signal, I mean, at the highest level Democrats, and that — is that changing in the government now, though, is Signal not going to be used or is it, is it going to be used going forward? Are you aware of it?

BONDI: I think Signal is a very safe way to communicate. I don't think foreign adversaries are able to hack Signal, as far as I know.

INGRAHAM: And maybe people just have to keep an eye on their contact list a little bit more carefully going forward. Attorney General Pam Bondi, you are up at what — what time did you get up this morning, two.

BONDI: Got to be about four.

INGRAHAM: Four.

BONDI: Four, four.

INGRAHAM: Was — you don't look any worse for the wear. Thank you very much for joining us. We appreciate it very much.

END

data.title: Singapore Post Completes Divestment of Australian Business; Shares Surge 6%
data.description:
Singapore Post completed the divestment of its Australian logistics business, Freight Management to Pacific Equity Partners, for AU$1.02 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The sale represents an SG$289.5 million gain for the Singaporean firm, the filing said.

Singapore Post said it will use the proceeds to reduce debt.

Shares of the company surged nearly 6% in recent trading.

data.title: Hangzhou Oxygen Plant's Profit Drops 24%, Operating Income Rises 3%
data.description:
Hangzhou Oxygen Plant Group's net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 dropped 24% to 922.4 million yuan, or 0.93 yuan per share, from 1.22 billion yuan, or 1.22 yuan per share, a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The Chinese industrial gas manufacturer's operating income rose 3.1% year on year to 13.7 billion yuan from 13.3 billion yuan, the filing said.

The company's shares dropped over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Indian bond yields dip as government's debt sale plan undershoots estimates
data.description:
Indian sovereign bond yields eased slightly in early deals on Friday after the government's lower-than-expected borrowing plan for the first half of next financial year aided sentiment.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.5904% as of 10:15 a.m. IST, compared with Thursday's close of 6.6022%.

The finance ministry, late on Thursday, said it plans to raise 8 trillion rupees ($93.41 billion) through bond sales between April and September, amounting to 54% of its gross borrowing plan for the financial year.

The market, however, was expecting New Delhi to borrow 56%-59% of its planned 14.82 trillion rupees for financial year 2026, up from 53% in the first half of the current fiscal year.

"There is bullish sentiment overall and we do not rule out some aggressive purchases towards the end of the session," a trader with a state-run bank said.

Friday also marks the end of the current financial year and investors could look to increase bond positions to prop up the balance sheet valuation of their treasury portfolios.

Yields move inversely to bond prices.

The recent drop in the benchmark bond yield has also narrowed its spread with its U.S. counterpart to a five-week low. By Thursday's close, the gap had eased to 225 basis points, the lowest since February 20, from around 250 bps three weeks ago.

The spreads could shrink further as the market expects the Reserve Bank of India to cut rates by as much as 75 bps this year. The central bank is widely expected to lower rates at its policy decision on April 9.

Foreign investors also ramped up purchases of Indian government bonds under the fully accessible route (FAR) in March amid anticipation of rate easing.

($1 = 85.6425 Indian rupees)

data.title: Ubisoft to Form New EUR4 Billion Subsidiary With Investment from China's Tencent
data.description:
Ubisoft Entertainment on Thursday announced the creation of a new subsidiary with an investment of 1.16 billion euros from Chinese conglomerate Tencent for a 25% stake.

The new subsidiary, with a pre-money enterprise value of 4 billion euros, will be based on the video game publisher's Assassin's Creed, Far Cry and Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six brands.

Ubisoft said the binding deal with Tencent follows a formal and competitive process to assess strategic options. The deal is expected to be completed before the end of 2025.

data.title: Longfor Group Posts Decline in Net Profit, Revenue Amid Property Slump
data.description:
(04:27 GMT) *Longfor Group 2024 Rev CNY127.47B Vs. CNY180.74B >0960.HK
(04:27 GMT) *Longfor Group 2024 Net CNY10.40B Vs. Net CNY12.85B >0960.HK
(04:29 GMT) *Longfor Group Declares Final Dividend of CNY0.10 >0960.HK
(04:54 GMT) Longfor Group Posts Decline in Net Profit, Revenue Amid Property Slump

By Jiahui Huang

Chinese property developer Longfor Group's net profit and revenue declined in 2024 amid a continued sector downturn in the world's second-largest economy.

The Beijing-based company's net profit fell 19% to 10.40 billion yuan, or equivalent of $1.43 billion, compared with the previous year. Its revenue dropped to 127.47 billion from 180.74 billion yuan in 2023.

Revenue from Longfor's core property development business fell 35% to 100.77 billion yuan.

While revenue from its property development and services segment declined last year, revenue from its investment property operation and property service segments rose 7.4% to CNY26.71 billion.

Longfor is one of the few Chinese developers that have been increasing profits from asset management and investment properties instead of homebuilding, as the country's property sector remains mired in a years-long downturn.

Looking ahead, Longfor said it will focus on reducing inventory in its property development business while replenishing land and improving its land bank structure.

The company declared a final dividend of CNY0.10 per share for the full year 2024.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Strandline Resources' Convening Period with Creditors Extended to July
data.description:
Strandline Resources , which is in voluntary administration, has been given until July 24 to convene a meeting with its creditors, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The new timeline is an extension from the original deadline of March 24. The extension was granted by the Supreme Court of Western Australia in consideration of the "size and complexity" of Strandline Resources, per the filing.

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on March 28
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening higher on Friday, with futures up 0.01%.

* SHELL: Companies, like Shell , planning a flagship offshore gas project between Venezuela and Trinidad and Tobago "have not slowed down" work, Trinidad Prime Minister Stuart Young said, despite the United States increasing pressure on sanctioned Venezuela and uncertainty over the extension of a license for the project.

* SHELL: Shell is discontinuing its solar and onshore wind power generation projects in Brazil as part of a "portfolio adjustment", the company said.

* BP: The head of BP's electric vehicle charging business BP Pulse, Richard Bartlett, has left the company, a BP spokesperson said, weeks after a strategy revamp to refocus on oil and gas.

* OIL: Oil prices held near one-month highs and were poised for a third weekly gain on a tightening global supply outlook after the U.S. imposed tariffs against countries buying oil and gas from Venezuela and placed restrictions on Iranian oil trade.

* METALS: Copper prices in London hit a nearly two-week low, as unease over looming U.S. tariffs weighed on sentiment.

* GOLD: Gold prices scaled a record peak as concerns over U.S. President Donald Trump's fresh tariff plans ignited fears of a global trade war, driving a rush towards the safe-haven allure of the precious metal.

* The UK blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed down 0.3% on Thursday, with mining stocks leading declines, after U.S. President Donald Trump announced new tariffs on all foreign-made vehicles and auto parts.

data.title: Mayar Swings to Profit in FY24; Revenue Grows
data.description:
Mayar Holding (SASE:9568) said Thursday it swung to a profit for full-year 2024, while revenue grew year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 17.2 million Saudi riyals, compared with a net loss of 41.9 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.14 riyal from a loss per share of 0.32 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed vertical transport device manufacturer's revenue was 446.7 million riyals, compared with 355 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: GQG Partners' Chairman Buys Chess Depositary Interests
data.description:
GQG Partners Chairman and Chief Investment Officer Rajiv Jain acquired 63,402 Chess Depositary Interests (CDIs) at AU$2.15 per CDI by way of an on-market trade, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Jain now holds about 2 million CDIs, per the filing. He also owns 2.07 billion common shares.

Shares of the company rose nearly 1% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery's Profit Jumps 30%, Operating Income Rises 7%
data.description:
Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery's net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 jumped 30% to 810.3 million yuan from 623.5 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Earnings per share climbed 32% year on year to 0.37 yuan from 0.28 yuan.

The Chinese brewery's operating income grew 6.6% to 5.73 billion yuan from 5.38 billion yuan in the prior year, the disclosure said.

The company's shares fell less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: China, HK stocks drop as investors remain wary of US tariff risks
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks fell broadly on Friday as investor sentiment cooled due to concerns that new U.S. tariffs could shake the global economy.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.71% at 3,349.68 points.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index was down 0.55%, with its financial sector sub-index lower by 0.39%, the consumer staples sector down 0.83%, the real estate index down 0.47% and the healthcare sub-index up 0.03%.

** Semiconductor (.CSI931865) and energy stocks dropped 2.1% and 1.5%, respectively, to lead the declines.

** The Chinese H-share index listed in Hong Kong, the Hang Seng China Enterprises Index , fell 1.1% to 8,582.09, while the Hang Seng Index (.HIS) was down 0.89% at 23,368.67.

** Hong Kong-listed tech giants lost 1.7%.

** Investors are still gauging the 25% tariff on auto imports announced by U.S. President Donald Trump and its impact on major exporting economies.

** Trump also plans to implement new reciprocal tariffs on several trading partners from April 2.

** Sentiment towards mainland Chinese stocks dropped with lower trading volumes, Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Laura Wang, said in a note on Friday.

** "We expect higher volatility amid tariff uncertainty but believe green shoots on earnings and liquidity warrant further index upside towards year-end," they said.

** The smaller Shenzhen index was down 0.73%, the start-up board ChiNext Composite index was weaker by 0.58% and Shanghai's tech-focused STAR50 index was down 0.75%​.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was weaker by 0.71% while Japan's Nikkei index was down 2.33%.

data.title: Singapore Manufactured Products, Domestic Supply Price Indices Fall in February
data.description:
Singapore's Manufactured Products Price Index fell 0.5% in February, reversing a 3.6% gain in January, the Department of Statistics reported Friday.

The oil index dropped 2.6%, while the non-oil index slipped 0.2%, weighed down by declines in machinery, transport equipment, and manufactured goods.

Year-on-year, the Manufactured Products Price Index rose 5.5%, following a 6.1% increase in January. The oil index fell by 5.3%, but the non-oil index gained by 7.5%, driven by higher prices for electrical machinery, food, and live animals.

Meanwhile, Singapore's Domestic Supply Price Index fell 0.7% in February after a 3.6% rise in January. The oil index declined 1.8%, while the non-oil index dropped 0.3%, driven by lower machinery, transport equipment, and manufactured goods prices.

Year-on-year, the Domestic Supply Price Index rose 5.1% after a 5.5% increase in January. The oil index fell by 4.5%, but the non-oil index climbed by 8.9%, driven by higher electrical machinery and food prices.

data.title: iFast Appoints Group CFO
data.description:
iFast will appoint Terence Lin Weide as the group chief financial officer, effective April 1, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Wiede will succeed Lim Kian Thong, who will be reaching retirement age.

data.title: Getlink Prices EUR600 Million Senior Green Notes
data.description:
Getlink priced on Thursday its offering of 600 million euros in 4.125% senior secured green notes due 2030 at a price equal to the principal amount.

The French tunnel and railway operator intends to close the issuance on April 4, subject to customary conditions.

Proceeds from the issuance will be used for the company's plan to redeem its existing 850 million euros of senior secured green notes that will mature in October.

data.title: Guocoland Places Dormant Subsidiary Under Voluntary Liquidation
data.description:
Guocoland placed dormant indirect wholly owned subsidiary, GLL EWI (HK) under member's voluntary liquidation, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The liquidation is not expected to have an impact on the company's earnings per share for the year ending June 30.

data.title: Shmoh Almadi Logs Lower FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Shmoh Almadi (SASE:9622) said Thursday that its net profit and revenue for full-year 2024 dropped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 9.5 million Saudi riyals, compared with 16 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 1.18 riyals from 2 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed food company's revenue was 131.6 million riyals, compared with 143 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Axiata Group: Appointed Patrick Forth as Independent Nonexecutive Director, Effective Immediately
FX option expiries for 28 March 10am New York cut
data.description:
There are a couple to take note of on the day, as highlighted in bold.

The first ones are for EUR/USD at the 1.0750 and 1.0800 levels once again. That should help to keep price action locked in once again, with the latter especially providing an extra layer of defense to any upside extensions on the day. For the week itself, the 100-week moving average at 1.0780 remains a crucial technical level to be mindful of.

Then, there is one for USD/JPY at the 151.00 level. If anything else, the expiries could provide a magnet for price action to weave in and about the figure level in the session ahead at least. That before we get to US trading later with the PCE price index release in focus as well. But all else being equal, the risk mood and bond yields will continue to be the bigger driver of trading sentiment for the pair today.

For more information on how to use this data, you may refer to this post here.

For the latest news on African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand weakened on Thursday as markets fretted over U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs due next week.

KENYA MARKETS

The Kenyan shilling was stable against the dollar on Thursday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) showed.

AFRICA MARKETS

The Kenyan, Ugandan, Nigerian and Ghanaian currencies are expected to be broadly stable against the dollar in the next week to Thursday, while Zambia's is seen under pressure, traders said.

KENYA IMF

The International Monetary Fund said on Thursday that Kenya's cash shortage requires a new spending strategy as it confirmed the government requested a new program.

SOUTH SUDAN BRITAIN

Britain on Thursday advised its citizens in South Sudan to leave the country following rising tensions that have brought the East African nation to the brink of renewed civil war.

CHAD MIGRATION

The U.S. embassy in Chad has suspended the issuance of most nonimmigrant visas for 90 days, the State Department said on Thursday, in the first known international travel restrictions of President Donald Trump's second term.

CONGO HUNGER

Twenty-eight million people face acute hunger in the Democratic Republic of Congo, a record for the country, driven by an escalating conflict between the government and Rwandan-backed rebels in the east, the United Nations said on Thursday.

SOUTH SUDAN SECURITY

The detention of South Sudan's First Vice President Riek Machar has effectively collapsed the peace deal that ended the 2013-2018 civil war, his party said on Thursday.

data.title: Arab Sea Information Systems' FY24 Net Loss Widens; Revenue Up
data.description:
Arab Sea Information Systems Co. (SASE:7201) said Thursday that its net loss for full-year 2024 widened, while revenue rose year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 17.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with a net loss of 9.8 million riyals earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.18 riyal from a loss per share of 0.09 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed software and IT services company's revenue was 43.3 million riyals, compared with 41.2 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Tibbiyah Swings to Loss in FY24; Revenue Climbs
data.description:
Arabian International Healthcare Holding Co. (SASE:9530), d/b/a Tibbiyah, said Thursday it swung to a loss in full-year 2024, while revenue rose year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 66.6 million Saudi riyals, compared with a profit of 38.1 million riyals earlier. Loss per share moved to 3.37 riyals from an EPS of 1.92 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed medical systems and equipment provider's revenue was 814.4 million riyals, compared with 806.9 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Palm Oil Rallies, Set for Modest Weekly Gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures jumped near 2.0% to around MYR 4,390 per tonne, marking the third session of gains.

The upturn followed news that U.S. oil and biofuel producers had agreed in principle to raise the mandate for renewable diesel and biodiesel.

Meanwhile, key buyer China increased palm oil purchases amid a trade dispute with Canada.

For the week, the contracts are set for their first rise in three weeks, up around 0.5% so far, supported by weather uncertainties.

Forecasts indicate a transition from La Niña to a neutral El Niño phase between March and May, which could impact output.

Meanwhile, Indonesia raised its crude palm oil reference price for April to USD 961.54 per metric ton while keeping a $124 per ton export tax.

However, gains were capped by concerns over labor shortages after the Eid ul-Fitr break next week.

On the export front, weak shipments are expected, as data from cargo surveyors showed a fall of between 8.1% to 8.5% from February during the March 1-25 period.

data.title: Global Unveiling of Inner Mongolia's 2025 Spring Tourism Event: Inviting the World to Enjoy a Culture and Nature Symphony of the Northern Frontier
data.description:
HOHHOT, China, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Recently, the Culture and Tourism Department of the Inner Mongolia autonomous region has announced a "Musical Tours in Inner Mongolia" series tourism product for 2025, which centered on the "culture of the northern frontier" will offer 219 cultural and tourist activities across six themes, inviting global travelers to immerse themselves in the vibrant spring of the grasslands. From the romantic sea of flowers along the Yellow River to the thrill of desert adventures, and from ancient intangible cultural heritage to modern artistic performances, this cultural and tourist feast from April through June weaves together nature, tradition, and innovation to unfold a beautiful tapestry of China's northern frontier to the world.

A Blossoming Canvas of Nature and Culture

As winter gives way to spring between late March and May, Inner Mongolia bursts into life along the "Great Bend" of the Yellow River, where pear and apricot blossoms paint the landscape in hues of pink and white. Visitors can wander the "Apricot Blossom and Flower Festival" trail in Tumed Right Banner of Baotou or hike through the ancient forests of the Greater Khingan Mountains to marvel at alpine azaleas blooming alongside remaining ice and snow. Meanwhile, featured activities jointly held by leagues and cities like Hohhot's Wusutu Apricot Flowers Festival and the ecological observation of Huazi fish (Leuciscus waleckii) migration in Chifeng transform "flower tourism" into immersive experiences, blending photography contests, open-air concerts, and nature education programs to celebrate the splendor of spring beyond visual enjoyment.

Deeper in the grasslands, a dialogue between heritage and modernity is unfolding quietly. The Ordos Horsehead Fiddle Symphony Orchestra will perform epic compositions against the vast horizon of the grasslands, while 30 nationwide tours of the "Spring Village Evening" bring intangible cultural heritage to life through folk songs and dances staged in scenic settings. From April 25 to 26, Rene Liu will headline two concerts in Hohhot, paired with travel packages offering tickets of scenic areas at a discount, themed hotel packages, and culinary guides, enticing music fans and tourists alike to explore the hidden gems of the region.

Contemporary Expression of Millennium-long Nomadic Culture

Spring in Inner Mongolia also heralds the renewal of nomadic culture. In Alxa's deserts, camel races and Mongolian wrestling competitions stir the spirit of adventure, while activities like sandboarding and stargazing camps allow visitors to connect with the magnificence of nature. On the Hulunbuir prairie, tourists can join shepherds in traditional tasks like wool shearing and milking, or participate in Xilingol's Mongolian Horse Super League, galloping across 200 kilometers of prairie and sand dunes.

Cultural inheritance also attracts great attention through immersive experiences. The live-action drama in Baotou, "Melodies in Baotou - Love for the Yellow River", reenacts ancient folk customs against the backdrop of the thawing Yellow River, while activities such as migratory bird observation on the Wuhai Lake merge ecological awareness with travel. Workshops led by intangible cultural inheritors invite visitors to craft horsehead fiddles and practice Mongolian embroidery, transforming museum artifacts into living traditions that resonate through touch and creativity.

Source: The Culture and Tourism Department of the Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region

Contact person: Mr. Hao, Tel: 86-10-63074558

data.title: Turkey detains lawyer of jailed Istanbul mayor, main opposition party says
data.description:
Turkish authorities detained a lawyer for President Tayyip Erdogan's biggest political rival, jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, an MP of the main opposition party, CHP, said on Friday.

Imamoglu, a CHP member who leads Erdoguan in some polls, was jailed on Sunday pending trial for graft, provoking the largest anti-government protests in a decade, which led in turn to mass arrests nationwide.

Mehmet Pehlivan, a lawyer who defended Imamoglu in the latest investigation, was detained "for fabricated reasons", CHP MP Turan Taskin Ozer said in a post on X, without giving details.

Imamoglu, who has been temporarily dismissed from his job because of the case, demanded the immediate release of his lawyer.

"As if the coup on democracy wasn't enough, they can't stand the victims of this coup defending themselves," Imamoglu said on X.
